Under pressure from his rival political party at home, Aristippus came to Cyrus and asked for payment for two thousand mercenaries to be employed for three months. This would enable him to gain the upper hand over his opponents. He next summoned the troops currently engaged in the siege of Miletus and called upon the exiles to follow him on his intended expedition. If he succeeded in achieving his goal, he promised that he would not rest until he had restored them to their native city.

Xenias arrived at Sardis along with a contingent of four thousand hoplites. Proxenus also joined with fifteen hundred hoplites and five hundred light-armed troops. Sophaenetus the Stymphalian brought one thousand hoplites, while Socrates the Achaean contributed five hundred hoplites. Cyrus, the king of the Cilicians, received a visit from his wife, Epyaxa. It was reported that she brought a substantial gift of money with her.
